title: 'Trump demands Iran's surrender' and 'UK voters say no to joining war'
description: The conflict in the Middle East continues to lead today's papers as the US calls for Iran's "unconditional surrender".
content: Most of Saturday's papers continue to focus on the US-Israel conflict with Iran. The Guardian leads on US President Donald Trump demanding Iran's "unconditional surrender" to help end the offensive launched a week ago. The US and Israel "carried out ... [2561 chars]

title: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
description: Ukraine has embarked on a programme to deploy armed robots on the battlefield against Russian forces.
content:
Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion, the war in Ukraine has developed into a high-tech conflict.
Swarms of spy and killer drones have set the skies of Ukraine abuzz, and uncrewed boats have crippled the Russian navy in the Black Sea.
Now,... [2786 chars]

title: Wolves 1-3 Liverpool: Arne Slot's side reach FA Cup quarter-finals
description: Despite a torrid Premier League campaign, could winning the FA Cup keep Liverpool manager Arne Slot in a job?
content:
Liverpool's Premier League title defence may well have ended months ago - but they still have a chance to save their season.
Three days after a painful stoppage-time defeat by Wolves left them sixth in the table, Arne Slot's side returned to Molineux... [4687 chars]

title: Robertson and Salah help Liverpool to redemption over Wolves in FA Cup tie
description: Liverpool beat Wolves 3-1 at Molineux to progress to the quarter-finals, three days after losing to the Premier League’s bottom side
content: This time around, Liverpool made no mistake at Molineux. They stayed on the road to Wembley, thanks to two players signed in 2017, beloved veterans with uncertain futures. Andy Robertson and Mohamed Salah scored the goals to ease the pain of Tuesday’... [4297 chars]

title: Obama, Biden, Clinton honour Jesse Jackson at funeral in Chicago
description: While praising Jackson, Obama made a thinly veiled dig at US President Donald Trump.
content:
Former US presidents, celebrities and thousands of members of the public have gathered to honour civil rights leader, the Reverend Jesse Jackson, who died last month.
